title: cereus aureus
intro: Cereus aureus, also known as the Golden Cereus or Devil's Head, is a species of cactus that belongs to the genus Cereus and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Cereus aureus include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its potential to treat respiratory problems and skin conditions. Cereus aureus can be prepared as an herbal infusion, tincture, or salve, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
